tool_input.summary: Relevant PDS holdings for radial temperature profiles in Jupiter’s dawnside magnetodisk come primarily from Galileo (PPI node): PLS for electron/ion moments and fitted thermal parameters, plus trajectory (to compute radial distance and local time) and optional MAG (to identify plasma sheet/magnetodisk intervals). For heavy ions, use PLS fitted ion-species parameters when available and/or Galileo HIC/EPD energetic heavy-ion products (composition/energy spectra) as a proxy/constraint.

candidates.title: Galileo Orbiter Plasma Science Experiment Jupiter Fitted Parameters Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Best starting point for temperatures vs. position in the Jovian plasma sheet/magnetodisk: provides per-interval PLS fit results for ion species (and quality flags), enabling electron/proton/ion thermal parameters to be binned by radial distance and local time (dawnside).

candidates.title: Galileo PLS Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Derived PLS ‘moments’ tables (density, bulk speed, temperature where available) suitable for computing temperature vs radial distance once merged with trajectory/local-time info.

candidates.title: Galileo PLS Jupiter Full Resolution Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated full-resolution PLS measurements if you need to recompute moments/temperatures or apply custom selection for dawnside magnetodisk intervals.

candidates.title: GO J PLS 5 RTS MOMENTS V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 counterpart for Galileo PLS moments; contains temperature/moment products used in many legacy Jovian plasma-sheet analyses.

candidates.title: GO J PLS 3 RDR FULLRES V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 full-resolution PLS RDR if you want to derive temperatures yourself (or reproduce published methodologies) before binning by radial distance.

candidates.title: Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides Galileo spacecraft trajectory in System III, JSE, and JSM coordinates—needed to compute radial distance (R), magnetic local time (to isolate dawnside), and to map observations into the magnetodisk.

candidates.title: GO J POS 6 SC TRAJ JUP COORDS V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 trajectory/position product (Jupiter coordinate sets) used to compute R and local time for pairing with PLS/HIC/EPD measurements.

candidates.title: Galileo Orbiter at Jupiter Magnetometer High Resolution Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Use MAG vectors to identify magnetodisk/plasma-sheet crossings (e.g., current sheet signatures) so temperature–radius profiles can be restricted to magnetodisk intervals and to dawnside sectors.

candidates.title: GO J MAG 3 RDR MAGSPHERIC SURVEY V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 magnetospheric-survey MAG product (legacy equivalent) for contextual selection of magnetodisk intervals when building temperature vs radial distance profiles.

candidates.title: Galileo Jupiter Heavy Ion Counter Derived Energetic Ion Composition Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic heavy-ion composition (e.g., C/O/S group ions) useful for the ‘heavy ion’ component in the magnetodisk; can be combined with thermal PLS fits or used to constrain heavy-ion populations vs radius on the dawnside.

candidates.title: GO J HIC 5 DDR ENERGETIC ION COMP V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 heavy-ion composition/energetic product (legacy equivalent) for analyzing radial trends in heavy-ion populations.

candidates.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic ion/electron measurements (keV–MeV) that can complement PLS thermal temperatures, especially where PLS coverage is limited; can help characterize electron/proton ‘temperatures’ via spectral fitting by radial distance/local time.

candidates.title: GO J EPD 2 REDR HIGHRES SECTOR V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 high-resolution EPD product (legacy equivalent) for energetic electrons/ions; useful for constructing dawnside radial profiles via spectral/temperature proxy fits.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Please note: The data in this bundle have been reviewed by the coauthors of Bagenal, et al. 2016 and their method and figures passed the journal's peer review. These data have also completed PDS peer review and are certified.
